(P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有馬町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tosen-jinja}} [[JA:湯泉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11563671}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in Hyogo Prefecture|other Yusen/Onsen shrines|Onsen Shrine}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{神社 | 名称=Yusen Shrine | 画像=[[ファイル:Tosen-jinja01s3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}1908 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社|大とうせんしんしや]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有馬町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tosen-jinja}} [[JA:湯泉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11563671}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in Hyogo Prefecture|other Yusen/Onsen shrines|Onsen Shrine}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{神社 | 名称=Yusen Shrine | 画像=[[ファイル:Tosen-jinja01s3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}1908 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]]

Onsen|zh|有馬溫泉|WD=Q4790673|1=Arima Onsen}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 2479014 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9140005000766 == Commons category (P373) == * Tosen-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有馬町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tosen-jinja}} [[JA:湯泉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11563671}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in Hyogo Prefecture|other Yusen/Onsen shrines|Onsen Shrine}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{神社 | 名称=Yusen Shrine | 画像=[[ファイル:Tosen-jinja01s3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}1908 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有馬町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tosen-jinja}} [[JA:湯泉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11563671}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in Hyogo Prefecture|other Yusen/Onsen shrines|Onsen Shrine}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{神社 | 名称=Yusen Shrine | 画像=[[ファイル:Tosen-jinja01s3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}1908 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:神戸市北区の建築物]]

Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有馬町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tosen-jinja}} [[JA:湯泉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11563671}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in Hyogo Prefecture|other Yusen/Onsen shrines|Onsen Shrine}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{神社 | 名称=Yusen Shrine | 画像=[[ファイル:Tosen-jinja01s3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}1908 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Kobe]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市北区有馬町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tosen-jinja}} [[JA:湯泉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11563671}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|shrine in Hyogo Prefecture|other Yusen/Onsen shrines|Onsen Shrine}} {{Citation needed|date=November 2019}} {{神社 | 名称=Yusen Shrine | 画像=[[ファイル:Tosen-jinja01s3200.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}1908 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 3|Q2931|WD=Q2931|1=October 3}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Yusen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Tosen-jinja02s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|Haiden]] '''Yusen Shrine''' (とうせんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Arima-chō|Q18700495|WD=Q18700495|1=Arimachō}}, {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Kobe)|Q1078814|WD=Q1078814|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe|Q48320|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is also read "Onsen Jinja" and may be written {{ill|lt=Onsen Shrine|Q11562963|WD=Q11562963|1=Onsen Shrines}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha major shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === History === Based on the tale that {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} discovered Arima Onsen after seeing three {{ill|lt=crows|Q388450|WD=Q388450|lt=crows|1=Crow}} soaking in red hot water to heal leg injuries, these two deities are enshrined here, and three birds are carved on the haiden. Together with Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto, newly worshiped from the era of restoration of Arima Onsen by priest Ninsei beginning in {{ill|lt=1191|Q19738|WD=Q19738|1=1191}} ({{ill|lt=Kenkyū|Q1193933|WD=Q1193933|1=Kenkyū}} 2), they are called the "Three Guardian Deities." Because the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' records visits by {{ill|lt=Emperor Jomei|Q329761|WD=Q329761|1=Emperor Jomei}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, and because it is listed among the 492 major shrines in the early-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, its high prestige in former times can be seen. Formerly the shrine buildings (built in the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 10 / {{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|WD=Q7823|1=1798}}) stood in the precincts of {{ill|lt=Onsen-ji (Kobe)|Q11562943|WD=Q11562943|lt=Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple}} at the foot of Mt. Atago, but in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 18) they were moved to the current site on the middle slope of Mt. Atago. It was also ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In front of Onsen-ji main hall there is a {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} and stone steps leading to the shrine buildings. === Shrine name === Some documents write it as Onsen Shrine, and proposed readings include "tōsen jinja," "tōzen jinja," and "yuno jinja," but today it is generally written "Yusen Shrine" and read "tōsen jinja." In the section for {{ill|lt=Arima District|Q11516836|WD=Q11516836|1=Arima district}} of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} in the 'Engishiki' Jinmyōchō, it is listed with {{ill|lt=Arima Shrine|Q11516720|WD=Q11516720|1=Arima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kōchi Shrine|Q11392062|WD=Q11392062|1=Kuchi Shrine}} as "Yusen Shrine (yuno jinja)" [http://www.arima-onsen.com/facility_info113.html]. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}, Kumano Kusumi-no-Mikoto (three guardian deities of Arima Onsen) === Precinct === * Honden * Haiden * Amatsu Shrine * Kunitsu Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} * Munakata Shrine * Koyasu-dō * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Myōken-dō|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property === * Silk painting {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} {{ill|lt=mandala|Q191431|WD=Q191431|1=Mandala}} === Events === * January 2 - {{ill|lt=Irizome-shiki|Q11388868|WD=Q11388868|1=Irizome-shiki}} * May 5 - Chinka Festival * July 3 - Mikoshi procession === Transportation === * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Arima-Onsen Station|Q4790676|WD=Q4790676|1=Arima Onsen Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kobe Electric Railway|Q836658|WD=Q836658|1=Kobe Electric Railway}} * 10 minutes on foot from Arima-Onsen Station of the {{ill|lt=Rokkō Arima Ropeway|Q7360054|WD=Q7360054|1=Rokkō Arima Ropeway}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Tosen-jinja}} *[http://www.tousen.or.jp/ Yusen Shrine] *[http://arimajinja.com/ Arima Shrine] {{Arima onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yusen Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:神戸市の重要文化財]] [[Category:有馬温泉郷]] [[Category:神戸市北区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Kobe]]
